pattern: An explicit stack of the not-yet-visited left spine turns recursive inorder traversal into a pausable object: each next() pops one node and pushes its right child's spine, amortizing to O(1).
hints: Flattening the whole tree in the constructor works but costs O(n) memory up front; the follow-up wants O(h).
Recursion pauses exactly at the chain of ancestors you have not visited — model that chain with your own stack.
Seed the stack with the path of left children from the root; next() pops a node, then walks the left spine of its right child. hasNext is just a non-empty check.

edge_inputs.description: classic mixed tree walked to exhaustion
right-skewed chain (spine rebuilt on every next)

stress.description: random BST fully drained with interleaved hasNext probes
